candidates.title: ロイシンが多い食品10選。筋肉づくりや加齢による筋力低下予防に役立つ食材とは？

candidates.text: 패션계는 ‘베일에 싸인’ 디자이너를 사랑해왔습니다. 공개적으로 얼굴을 드러낸 적이 한 번도 없는 마르탱 마르지엘라가 지금까지도 전설로 회자되는 것만 봐도 알 수 있죠. 패션계가 미스터리 못지않게 사랑하는 것이 바로 극적인 ‘컴백 스토리’입니다. 인종차별 논란 이후 재기에 성공한 존 갈리아노, 그리고 페리 엘리스에서 해고당한 뒤 루이 비통 최초의 크리에이티브 디렉터로 선임된 마크 제이콥스를 떠올려보세요. 2011년 발망을 떠난 […]
candidates.title: 가장 미스터리한 디자이너, 크리스토프 데카르넹이 돌아왔다

provenance.label: 真实采集
provenance.notes: 来自 Visual Radar 官方 Feed 或公开栏目页采集器。

candidates.text: スペイン優勝で幕を閉じたサッカーW杯。選手たちの熱戦の裏で、スパイクやユニフォーム供給を巡るスポーツメーカーの戦いに目を向けた人も多いのでは？ FASHIONSNAPでは、世界のスポーツメーカー17社の2025年度業績*をもとに売上高ランキングを作成。首位ナイキ（NIKE）、2位アディダス（adidas）のワンツーフィニッシュは1990年代以降変わらないが、背後では大きな変化が起きている。ナイキが踊り場を迎える中で、中国最大手のアンタ（ANTA）は3位の座を確固たるものにし、同時にオン（On）やアメアスポーツ（Amer Sports）、アシックス（ASICS）といった高価格帯市場で存在感を強めるメーカー群が急成長。かつて3位争いを演じたプーマ（PUMA）は主要メーカーで唯一の2ケタ減収となるなど、勢力図が大きく塗り替わっている。 *2025年6月〜2026年5月内の決算期で比較 このコンテンツは FASHIONSNAP が配信しています。
candidates.title: ナイキは踊り場、アディダスに迫る中国陣営 世界スポーツメーカー売上高ランキング2025

candidates.text: 아방가르드하면서도 실용적인 샌들이요! 지난주 휴가 기간 동안 교토에 다녀왔습니다. 극한의 여름 더위 때문에 일본의 대구라 불리는 데 비해 비가 자주 내렸고 시원했죠. 덕분에 거리는 운동화파와 샌들파로 나뉘어 있었습니다. 직장인이 아닌 남자는 대부분 운동화였고 여성들은 운동화와 샌들이었지만, 누구도 오니츠카타이거나 아디다스를 신지는 않았습니다. (외국인들만 오로지 오니츠카타이거를 신고 있었습니다!) 그래서인지 눈에 띈 건 독특하고 실용적인 샌들이었어요. 유명하다는 카페에 […]
candidates.title: 일본에 갔더니, 이런 샌들을 많이 신더군요!

provenance.label: 真实采集
provenance.notes: 来自 Visual Radar 官方 Feed 或公开栏目页采集器。

candidates.text: ​Video Art Visions: Romain Gavras & Surkin - Baikonur Imagining futures glimpsed from the margins, shaped less by collapse than by distortion, French filmmaker Romain Gavras and musician-producer Surkin build a multimedia world around peripheral dystopias for GENER8ION: Visions of 2034 – a major exhibition at London’s 180 Studios . The first of ten audiovisual works, observing familiar yet skewed environments, their experimental film Baikonur documents the world’s oldest space launch facility, the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan – a shadow of the Soviet era that remains under Russian control. Reflecting on how culture is consumed, processed, and forgotten, the video piece sits within a wider concept examining mutations between the reflexes of pleasure and the machinery of control – in response to cultural theorist Jean Baudrillard’s concept of simulation. Meditating on the spaceport’s unchanged infrastructure, and the moment a rocket launches into space, Gavras and Surkin consider the philosophy of freedom from a site that occupies a strange space in history – existing as a vision of what survives, and what doesn’t in an uncertain future. What first brought you to Kazakhstan? Romain Gavras: It’s the second time I went. I love the "Stans" – the layers of history, ancient and recent, in that part of the world are worn on the sleeves How did you land on the idea for the film? I was originally scouting for a secret project. We went with my line producer Guillaume Le François and my DOP André Chemetoff and when we got there we couldn’t stop shooting. What drew your attention to the Baikonur Cosmodrome? I’ve always been fascinated by it. It’s a Russian built enclave in the middle of the Kazakh desert, only made to support the Cosmodrome – like an ex-Soviet Truman Show for space exploration. At the time, it was the only place on Earth where they launched humans into space, so even our astronauts had to use the Russian infrastructures of Roscosmos to get up to space. It was also the only place in the world where you were allowed to film a rocket launched from only a kilometer away – Cape Canaveral, for example, is 8km. How did you go about creating a sense of dystopia? I didn’t really have to, it’s there. Built in the ‘50s , nothing has really moved, there is an amazing sense of purpose in the architecture of it, a sense that the future was going to be glorious – then time froze everything in that period. When I shot it, the only humans going to space were launched from there and every technological aspect is from the ‘70s – the bus the astronauts arrive at the rocket in, the train the rocket travels in. It’s fascinating. How did you guide the visual approach? It was really meant to be documentation for my own research, so it is very removed from a strong intention, very matter of fact and photo-like. How has your creative relationship with Surkin evolved? We’ve known each other since forever, and always liked to work together and talk about ideas about music and images. Can you talk us through some of the film’s themes, and how this relates to the exhibition as a whole? We open the exhibition with it. I feel it gives context about how history moves and the sentiment of the exhibit as a whole, which is constructed as a retrospective about a time in the future. Within this body of work, what message did you want the film to communicate? I’m not keen on straightforward messages, but I like the idea of creating a feeling, and the feelings I have about the future are dual. One take is that no matter what happens, there is still going to be poetry and beauty in the world, and kids will be kids doing silly things no matter the environment. My other take is that we all know that we’re dancing on the edge of a volcano. GENER8ION: Visions of 2034 will be on view at 180 Studios in London until 30 August 2026.
candidates.title: ​Video Art Visions: Romain Gavras & Surkin - Baikonur

instructions: 第一阶段只做内容价值评分与淘汰，不进行完整中文翻译。
第二阶段仅对高价值候选生成中文标题、摘要、关键词和编辑观察。
将标准化结果写入 data/visual_radar_agent_output.json。
完成 output 文件后运行 pnpm agent:import 导入分析结果。
